column1: # .zap/rules.tsv — OWASP ZAP baseline known false-positive allowlist
#   - IGNORE: Known false-positive; waiver requires security-team review
#             (CODEOWNERS rule). Each entry MUST include CVE/ZAP-ID + reason.
#   - WARN:   Track but do not block PR. Default for informational items.
#   - FAIL:   Override default WARN to make it block PRs.
# Waiver format: <ZAP-rule-id> TAB IGNORE TAB CVE:<id> waived-until:<date> ticket:<url> reason:<text>
# DO NOT add waivers for HIGH or CRITICAL findings without a security-team
# review in a GitHub PR with CODEOWNERS approval. See docs/security/scan-policy.md.
# Example waivers (uncomment and fill in when needed):

column3: Strict-Transport-Security header: HSTS not seen on all responses — Caddy sets it; ZAP scans HTTP redirect which lacks it. Waived. ticket:https://github.com/org/vici2/issues/XXX
Server leaks version — suppressed via Caddy `-Server` header; ZAP may still see it on error pages.
Content Security Policy (CSP) — informational check; CSP is set via Caddy header directive.
